title: Merck — RSV — briefing Q3-2025
html:
Enflonsia Briefing: Q3 2025 (Jul-Sep 2025)

Governing Thought

Enflonsia entered the market in the most favorable conditions possible -- FDA approval (June 9), ACIP recommendation with no preferential treatment vs. Beyfortus (June 26), and commercial launch (July) ahead of the 2025-26 RSV season. The window between approval and first season orders was extremely tight, giving Merck roughly 3 months to establish distribution, train reps, and secure stocking commitments against an entrenched incumbent.

Key Events This Quarter

Date Event Significance
Jun 9, 2025 FDA approves Enflonsia First direct competitor to Beyfortus in infant passive RSV immunization
Jun 9, 2025 Sanofi announces Beyfortus supply acceleration Defensive move: tripled capacity, doubled manufacturing sites since 2023
Jun 26, 2025 ACIP recommends Enflonsia No preferential rec between nirsevimab and clesrovimab -- critical legitimacy
Jul 2025 Enflonsia commercial launch / ordering begins Merck races to get product into practices before RSV season (Oct)
Aug-Oct 2025 Digital Tracker Wave 1 (Q3 '25) fielding Baseline digital landscape captured

Market Context at Launch

Beyfortus Position

  • 2 full seasons of commercial experience
  • $2.1B in 2025 revenue (growing 9.5% YoY despite Enflonsia entry)
  • Supply constraints resolved: tripled capacity, reservation program working
  • 6M+ infants immunized globally with 40+ RWE studies
  • Second-season indication already approved

Enflonsia Position at Launch

  • Zero market presence, zero RWE
  • Key differentiators established in label: single 105 mg dose regardless of weight, 30-month shelf life, 48-hour room temp stability
  • Price set at parity: $556/dose (matching Beyfortus exactly)
  • Rep training and deployment underway
  • FDA approval + ACIP rec in hand

Competitive Timing

Enflonsia's launch compressed the typical pharma launch cycle. In most categories, there are months between approval and commercial relevance. In seasonal RSV, Merck had ~3 months between FDA approval (June 9) and the start of RSV season (October). This meant:

  • Reps were detailing a brand-new product to HCPs who had already placed Beyfortus orders
  • Digital campaigns had to launch immediately (banner ads referencing FDA approval began in October)
  • Email campaigns focused on ordering logistics and CDC contract availability

Competitor Earnings Intelligence

Revenue Snapshot — Q3 2025

Company Brand Q3 2025 Revenue Key Signal
Sanofi/AZ Beyfortus (nirsevimab) ~$200M Off-season (RSV season Oct-Mar); strong first-season uptake, 60%+ infant coverage in accessible markets
Pfizer Abrysvo (RSVpreF) ~$50M Maternal vaccine uptake slower than expected; narrow gestational window limiting adoption
GSK Arexvy (RSV, older adults) ~$150M First-mover in older adult RSV; two-season data strengthening position
Enflonsia Launching FDA approved June 9; ACIP rec June 26; commercial launch July 2025

Sanofi/AstraZeneca — Beyfortus (~$200M)

Sanofi Q3 2025 earnings commentary:

  1. Beyfortus launched for the 2024-25 RSV season with strong uptake despite supply constraints. Sanofi noted that supply limitations restricted access in the first commercial season, but among markets where the product was available, 60%+ infant coverage was achieved — a remarkably fast adoption curve for a new immunization category.

  2. Supply acceleration announced defensively on Enflonsia's FDA approval date (June 9). Sanofi tripled manufacturing capacity and doubled manufacturing sites since 2023. The timing — same day as Enflonsia's approval — signals that Sanofi views Merck as a credible competitive threat and is preemptively addressing the one vulnerability (supply) that could open the door.

  3. Off-season revenue (~$200M) establishes the baseline for seasonal dynamics. RSV immunizations are heavily seasonal (Oct-Mar). Q3 revenue reflects stocking and catch-up doses rather than peak demand. The seasonal pattern will be important for interpreting Enflonsia's launch trajectory.

Implication for Merck: Beyfortus entered the 2025-26 season with 2 years of commercial experience, 6M+ infants immunized, and resolved supply constraints. Enflonsia is entering against an incumbent that has already addressed its biggest weakness. Merck's competitive window depends on operational differentiators (dosing simplicity, shelf life) rather than supply gaps.

Pfizer — Abrysvo (~$50M)

Pfizer Q3 2025 earnings commentary:

  1. Maternal vaccine uptake significantly slower than expected. Concerns about the narrow gestational window (32-36 weeks) and pediatrician preference for monoclonal antibodies over maternal vaccination are limiting Abrysvo's infant RSV strategy.

  2. Pediatricians prefer monoclonal approach (Beyfortus/Enflonsia) over maternal vaccination. This is a structural validation of Enflonsia's mechanism — the market is choosing direct infant immunization over indirect protection via the mother.

  3. Pfizer pivoting Abrysvo messaging toward older adults. The older adult RSV indication is becoming Abrysvo's primary commercial focus, effectively conceding the infant market to monoclonal antibodies.

Implication for Merck: Abrysvo's struggles validate Enflonsia's monoclonal antibody approach. The infant RSV market is consolidating around Beyfortus and Enflonsia, not fragmenting across mechanisms. This simplifies Merck's competitive framing: the battle is Enflonsia vs. Beyfortus, not Enflonsia vs. the field.

GSK — Arexvy (~$150M)

GSK's Arexvy is a vaccine for older adults (60+) and does not directly compete with Enflonsia in the infant RSV space. However, Arexvy's first-mover position and growing evidence base in older adults (~$150M off-season) demonstrates the commercial viability of RSV prevention broadly.

Implication for Merck: Arexvy is not a competitive threat to Enflonsia — different target population, different mechanism. Its relevance is as a market validator: RSV prevention is an established, growing category across age groups.

Synthesis: What the Combined Picture Tells Us

  1. The infant RSV market is a two-product race. Beyfortus vs. Enflonsia is the only competitive dynamic that matters. Abrysvo's maternal approach is losing, and older adult vaccines (Arexvy, Abrysvo, mResvia) serve a different population.

  2. Beyfortus enters the 2025-26 season from a position of strength — resolved supply, 60%+ coverage, 6M+ infants of RWE. Enflonsia must compete on operational differentiation (dosing, shelf life, room temp stability) because the efficacy and safety perception gap favors the incumbent.

  3. Sanofi's same-day supply announcement is a competitive signal. When an incumbent preemptively addresses vulnerabilities on the day a competitor is approved, it signals respect for the threat. Enflonsia's launch is being taken seriously.

Digital Tracker Insights (Aug-Oct 2025 Baseline)

Browsing Behavior

  • RSV content = ~2% of total healthcare-related browsing
  • Within RSV content, Beyfortus occupied more browsing time than Enflonsia
  • Top websites: UpToDate, CDC, AAP publications, AAFP
  • HCPs browsing Beyfortus sites explored more pages; Enflonsia visits concentrated on product information homepages

Paid Search

  • ~50% of HCP paid search exposures came from Beyfortus keyword searches
  • Enflonsia had low reach on its own branded keywords (2% of searches) but captured share on Beyfortus (15% of searches) and general RSV terms (12%)
  • Beyfortus achieved broader keyword visibility across both branded and generic RSV searches

Emails

  • HCPs received comparable share of emails from both brands
  • Enflonsia emails focused on ordering information and CDC contract updates (operational)
  • Beyfortus emails focused on clinical data and seasonal awareness (clinical/educational)
  • Enflonsia email drove 38% of visits to Enflonsia product sites

Banner Ads

  • Extremely sparse: 87% of HCPs saw zero market-basket RSV ads
  • Beyfortus had 10% reach vs. 3% for Enflonsia
  • Enflonsia's primary ad: "NOW APPROVED -- Available to order in July 2025" (70% of Enflonsia ad exposures)
  • Beyfortus's primary ad: Safety/indication messaging (53% of Beyfortus ad exposures)

Client Financial Signals — Merck Q3 2025 Earnings

What Merck management said on the Q3 2025 call:

  1. Enflonsia was mentioned as a "key pipeline asset for 2025-2026 launches" — pre-approval at the time of Q3 earnings. No specific revenue guidance was provided.
  2. The RSV opportunity was framed as part of Merck's "beyond Keytruda" growth narrative. Management positioned Enflonsia alongside IDVYNSO and other pipeline assets as evidence that Merck's revenue diversification strategy is active.
  3. No discussion of competitive dynamics against Beyfortus/Sanofi. Management treated Enflonsia as a pipeline asset rather than a commercial competitor, reflecting the pre-approval status.

Key analyst Q&A signals:

  1. Analysts did not ask RSV-specific questions on the Q3 2025 call. The earnings discussion was overwhelmingly Keytruda-centric, with secondary attention on GARDASIL's China decline.
  2. The absence of RSV questions suggests the Street does not yet view Enflonsia as a material revenue contributor — which creates an expectations advantage if the launch trajectory exceeds modest projections.

So What for ZoomRx

  1. Baseline established -- Q3 '25 digital data provides the pre-competitive baseline. All subsequent waves can measure Enflonsia's digital penetration against this starting point.
  2. Beyfortus category dominance confirmed -- Higher browsing time, broader search visibility, more diverse email content. This is the mountain Enflonsia must climb.
  3. Enflonsia's paid search strategy is smart -- Bidding on competitor and generic keywords reaches category-seeking HCPs who haven't yet committed. This is the right digital strategy for a new entrant.
  4. Email content gap -- Enflonsia's operational/ordering emails are necessary but insufficient. Beyfortus's clinical/educational emails build deeper engagement.
